blocks.body: Would rather shows preference: what we would prefer to do ourselves or what we would prefer someone else to do.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"Would rather helps us say that we prefer one option to another. The pattern works for our own action and for situations where we want someone else to behave differently.","rules":[{"label":"MY PREFERENCE","color":"emerald","text":"For your own action, use would rather + base verb.","example":"I'd rather stay home tonight."},{"label":"NEGATIVE FORM","color":"blue","text":"For the negative, use would rather not + base verb.","example":"I'd rather not talk about it now."},{"label":"ANOTHER PERSON","color":"amber","text":"When you want someone else to do something, use would rather + someone + past simple.","example":"I'd rather you came earlier tomorrow."}]}

{"title":"1. Form","intro":"These are the most useful patterns:","items":[{"label":"Your own action","title":"would rather + base verb","subtitle":"I'd rather take the train."},{"label":"Negative form","title":"would rather not + base verb","subtitle":"We'd rather not wait outside."},{"label":"Another person","title":"would rather + someone + past simple","subtitle":"I'd rather he stayed here."},{"label":"Question form","title":"Would you rather ...?","subtitle":"Would you rather go now or later?"}]}

{"title":"2. Main uses","sections":[{"label":"Personal preference","color":"emerald","description":"We compare two options and show which one we prefer.","examples":[{"en":"I'd rather walk than wait for the bus.","ua":"I'd rather walk than wait for the bus."},{"en":"She'd rather study at home tonight.","ua":"She'd rather study at home tonight."}]},{"label":"Would rather someone did","color":"blue","description":"This pattern softly shows what action we want from another person.","examples":[{"en":"I'd rather you didn't mention this to Alex.","ua":"I'd rather you didn't mention this to Alex."},{"en":"We'd rather the children stayed inside.","ua":"We'd rather the children stayed inside."}]},{"label":"Questions about choice","color":"amber","description":"Would you rather...? is a polite way to offer a choice.","examples":[{"en":"Would you rather sit by the window?","ua":"Would you rather sit by the window?"},{"en":"Would you rather meet on Friday or Saturday?","ua":"Would you rather meet on Friday or Saturday?"}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Adding to after would rather.","wrong":"I'd rather to stay home.","right":"I'd rather stay home.","hint":"After would rather, use the base verb without to."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the present simple after someone.","wrong":"I'd rather you come tomorrow.","right":"I'd rather you came tomorrow.","hint":"After would rather + someone, we usually use the past simple."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Leaving out would in normal preference statements.","wrong":"I rather stay home today.","right":"I'd rather stay home today.","hint":"In natural English, this meaning usually needs would rather."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Would rather + base verb = my preference.","Would rather not + base verb = the option I do not want.","Would rather + someone + past simple = the action I want from another person.","Do not use to after would rather."]}
